description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages lint, tests, dist, and deploy. In the dist stage define archlinux, debian, fedora, and ubuntu jobs, each using its respective image (archlinux, debian, fedora, ubuntu) and a before_script that installs make, pass, grep, git and other packages (pacman -Syu --noconfirm --noprogressbar make grep pass git jq kcov; apt-get update -q && apt-get install -y make pass grep wget git xz-utils; dnf -y install make gpg pass grep git findutils --setopt=install_weak_deps=False; apt-get update -q && apt-get install -y make pass grep git). Each of these jobs runs the script 'make tests' as defined in the .distribution template. Add a tests job in the tests stage using the archlinux image, with a before_script that runs pacman -Syu --noconfirm --noprogressbar make grep pass git jq kcov, then executes 'make tests COVERAGE=true' and moves the generated coverage directory with 'mv /tmp/tests/pass-update/kcov kcov/', and stores the kcov/ directory as a 2‑day artifact. Add a shellcheck job in the lint stage using the koalaman/shellcheck-alpine image that runs 'shellcheck --shell=bash update.bash tests/commons tests/results'. Finally, add a pages job in the deploy stage that depends on the tests job, moves the kcov/ folder to public/ with 'mv kcov/ public/', publishes the public/ directory as a 2‑day artifact, and runs only when the commit ref name equals the default branch.
